LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO PUBLIC ADMINISTRATOR'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO JANUARY 28, 2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: LAVENNA WARD, CSR # 7077
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF JAN-30-2013 CONTINUED TO MAR-20-2013 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.
MINI MINUTES FOR JAN-28-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO PUBLIC ADMINISTRATOR'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AND OVERRULED IN PART. THE DEMURRER ON STATUTE OF FRAUDS, FAILURE TO STATE FACTS SUFFICIENT TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION, STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS, AND UNCERTAINTY GROUNDS IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO CLARIFY WHAT CAUSES OF ACTION HE INTENDS TO ALLEGE IN THE COMPLAINT AND FACTS SHOWING THAT THE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE NOT BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF FRAUDS OR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. THE DEMURRER TO THE ATTORNEY FEES IS OVERRULED. THIS ISSUE SHOULD HAVE BEEN RAISED THROUGH A MOTION TO STRIKE, NOT DEMURRER, BUT IN ANY EVENT PLAINTIFF REMOVED THE REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND CONCEDES THAT HE IS NOT SEEKING SUCH FEES. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO PUBLIC ADMINISTRATOR'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF HAS PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO ALLEGE EACH CAUSE OF ACTION. WHETHER EQUITABLE ESTOPPEL APPLIES IS A QUESTION OF FACT THAT IS NOT THE SUBJECT OF A DEMURRER. AS PLED, THE CLAIMS ARE NOT BARRED BY THE APPLICABLE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. ORDER SENT BY EMAIL WAS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD KRAMER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-22-2013 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO PUBLIC ADMINISTRATOR's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to allege each cause of action. Whether equitable estoppel applies is a question of fact that is not the subject of a demurrer. As pled, the claims are not barred by the applicable statute of limitations. Order sent by email was signed in open court. Judge: Richard Kramer; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-28-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO PUBLIC ADMINISTRATOR's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained in part with leave to amend and overruled in part. The demurrer on statute of frauds, failure to state facts sufficient to state a cause of action, statute of limitations, and uncertainty grounds is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff is given leave to amend to clarify what causes of action he intends to allege in the complaint and facts showing that the causes of action are not barred by the statute of frauds or statute of limitations. The demurrer to the attorney fees is overruled. This issue should have been raised through a motion to strike, not demurrer, but in any event Plaintiff removed the request for attorney fees and concedes that he is not seeking such fees. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
